expectations.text: Output has at least 3 markdown H2/H3 sections
expectations.evidence: 7 H2 sections: How RSC Works Internally, Server/Client Boundary, Data Fetching Patterns, When to Use, Real-World Usage, Key Takeaways, Sources

expectations.text: Explains the server-side rendering model or how RSC differs from client components
expectations.evidence: Detailed explanation of React Flight wire format, RSC payload, streaming pipeline, and 'runs once on server' model

expectations.text: Mentions Next.js or another RSC-supporting framework
expectations.evidence: ## Real-World Usage section covers Next.js App Router as primary implementation, plus React Router v7, TanStack Start

expectations.text: Provides guidance on when to use vs avoid RSC
expectations.evidence: ## When to Use Server Components with explicit 'Use Server Components When' / 'Use Client Components When' lists
6 Common Gotchas listed: context, serialization, 'use client' viral, third-party libs, mental model shift, early adoption

notes: Focused, well-synthesized output covering the React Flight wire format specifically (unusual depth), data fetching waterfall problem with concrete solutions, and current adoption stats (29%). Sources include primary React docs and key community posts. Tighter than baseline — 178 lines vs 344 but better focused on the user's actual question.
